title: Keep A-B transport bounded to the active clip and loop window
summary: 0.6.35 makes A-B behave more cleanly during transport: moving to another movie clears the current A-B loop state, and scrub seeks stay inside the active A-B window instead of jumping outside it.
changes: Clear A-B state when manual next or previous transport moves the player to a different playlist item.
Clamp shared seek targets into the active A-B loop range when A-B is enabled and valid.
Leave playlist truth, nav behavior, surfaces, and deployment seams unchanged.
reasoning: A-B should belong to the current movie, not leak forward when the operator intentionally navigates to another one.
If A-B is active, scrubbing outside the active window creates a confusing fight between the slider and the loop rule.
Both behaviors belong in the shared transport seam so direct and hosted paths keep one rule.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: After setting A-B on a movie, pressing next or previous to load another movie clears the old A-B state.
While A-B is enabled, scrub seeks stay within the active A-B range.
knownIssues: This slice does not change slideshow, volume gestures, spatial audio, or the remaining grab-nav polish.
